sample_content: 6586185_DRV, 6WM, LHD, SAB, ARMREST, LUMBAR, HEATER, FABRIC CURITIBA BITON VP NAFTA
test_regulation: Test performed according to the Stellantis test procedure B32-3210
test_object: Test applicable on K0 complete front seat
test_fixture: K0 front seat crash fixture for metal validation (simulating podium as rigid) K0 foot rest K0 B-pilar
sled_pulse: STM file appendix 1 generic pulse for front impact (all seat rows) seat sizing generic pulse: Rexroth => J050271 Seattle safety => J100357
test_setup: Method how to calculate the pelvis H-point by tracking the femur: Initial position of the thigh model: Maximum forward position of the thigh model: Before test: Adjust the dummy in order to fullfill Hpoint and pelvis angle corridor. Attach on the femur plate the targets P and F in order that Hpoint + target P + Target F are on one line Measure the distance A between the H point and P femur pattern and angle alpha by recording with faro arm the Hpoint and target P and F coordinates After test: By using tracking software Measure on the first frame of the movie the angle Alpha formed by the straight line passing through the center of two femur patterns P and F and horizontal. Measure at the maximum advanced position of the pelvis the angle Beta formed by the straight line passing through the center of the two femur patterns P and F and horizontal. Measuring ΔXp at advanced X position of the point P Measuring ΔZp at advanced Z position of the point P Calculate the coordinates of H advanced such as: Hx= A cos α + ΔXP – A cos β Hz= A sin α + ΔZP – A sin β
camera_setup: 1 - Mid Left 90° | 2 - Mid Right 90° | 3 - Top camera | 4 - Front Left 45°
seat_back_angle: Backrest to be adjusted in design position => angle from recliner pivot point to headrest bezel exit centre point = 14.5°
head_restraint: 1 - Adjusted to the height of the head => Z-backset closest to 0mm 2 - Adjust the headrest in X to the fully rearward position
